title: Squash-merge divergence: denied force-push, recover via reset + cherry-pick + merge-base move
problem: After a squash-merge, the session branch's tip was the pre-squash commit. A normal push was non-fast-forward and a force-push was DENIED by policy.
state: Local branch diverged from the squashed remote tip; the only mechanical fix (force-push) is blocked, so the divergence cannot be overwritten.
solution: Fetch and reset to the remote tip, cherry-pick the new commits, then merge main to move the merge-base. This yields a clean fast-forward push and a minimal PR diff.
ail_v1:
[R:tool] %0 = fetch(remote="origin", ref="main")
         %1 = read(path=.git/refs/heads/main)
[R:think] %2 = combine(%0, %1)
[R:risk] %3 = check_permission(verb="push", tool=`gh`) ; state="non_fast_forward"
         %4 = block(action="force_push", reason="policy_denied")
[R:tool] %5 = write(path=.git/HEAD, base=%0)
         %6 = read(path=cherry_pick.log)
         %7 = merge(branch="main", base=%5)
[R:bench] %8 = build(target=%7, kind="rust") ; duration_s=18
          gate(ci:pass) ∵ %8
[R:artifact] gate(no_cjk:pass) ∵ %7
[R:commit] %9 = merge(pr=205) ∵ %8
arp:
[STRIDE: fetch_remote → reset_to_tip → cherry_pick → merge_main → push]
[T:80]
[R:tool] %0 = fetch(remote="origin", ref="main") [+]
         %1 = read(path=.git/refs/heads/main)
[R:think] %2 = combine(%0, %1)
[T:60]
[R:risk] %3 = check_permission(verb="push", tool=`gh`) ; state="non_fast_forward" [-]
         %4 = block(action="force_push", reason="policy_denied")
[BRANCH: force_push | reset_then_cherry_pick; condition: force_push_denied_by_policy]
[R:tool] %5 = write(path=.git/HEAD, base=%0)
         %6 = read(path=cherry_pick.log) [+]
         %7 = merge(branch="main", base=%5)
[T:30]
[R:bench] %8 = build(target=%7, kind="rust") ; duration_s=18 [+]
          gate(ci:pass) ∵ %8
[R:artifact] gate(no_cjk:pass) ∵ %7
[T:10]
[R:commit] %9 = merge(pr=205) ∵ %8 [+]
read: The problem was a squash-merge that left the session branch pointing at the pre-squash commit, so a normal push was rejected as non-fast-forward and a force-push was denied by policy. The state was a hard divergence with no overwrite path. The solution was to fetch and reset to the remote tip, cherry-pick the new commits onto it, and merge main to advance the merge-base, producing a clean fast-forward push and a minimal PR diff.

title: Independent test-author writing blind from the spec caught two unenforced §3 rules
problem: A test-author writing tests blind from the spec (no implementation in view) flagged two §3 rules that were not actually enforced: a silently-passing R:risk and an R:commit allowed under a live fail-gate.
state: The guardrail accepted graphs the spec forbids: an R:risk that emitted neither a fail-gate nor a mitigation, and an R:commit reachable while a gate(*:fail) was live.
solution: The blind tests pinned the §3 contract; the R:risk no-silent-pass rule and the fail-gate freeze were enforced, and both behaviours were locked by regression tests before committing.
ail_v1:
[R:tool] %0 = read(path=docs/ailish/2026-06-14-ailish-v1-rfc.md)
         %1 = write(path=tests/guardrail.rs)
[R:think] %2 = combine(%0, %1)
[R:review] %3 = join(%0, %2)
           gate(parse:pass) ∵ %3
[R:risk] %4 = check_permission(verb="commit", tool=`gh`) ; rule="no_silent_pass"
         %5 = block(action="silent_risk", reason="must_gate_or_mitigate")
[R:bench] %6 = test(target=%2) ; pass=2
          gate(ci:pass) ∵ %6
[R:artifact] gate(english:pass) ∵ %2
[R:commit] %7 = commit(scope="guardrail_tests") ∵ %6
arp:
[STRIDE: read_spec → author_blind_tests → flag_gaps → mitigate → commit]
[T:75]
[R:tool] %0 = read(path=docs/ailish/2026-06-14-ailish-v1-rfc.md) [+]
         %1 = write(path=tests/guardrail.rs)
[R:think] %2 = combine(%0, %1)
[T:55]
[R:review] %3 = join(%0, %2)
           gate(parse:pass) ∵ %3 [!]
[R:risk] %4 = check_permission(verb="commit", tool=`gh`) ; rule="no_silent_pass" [-]
         %5 = block(action="silent_risk", reason="must_gate_or_mitigate")
[BRANCH: accept_silent_risk | require_gate_or_mitigation; condition: section3_rule_unenforced]
[R:bench] %6 = test(target=%2) ; pass=2 [+]
          gate(ci:pass) ∵ %6
[R:artifact] gate(english:pass) ∵ %2
[T:15]
[R:commit] %7 = commit(scope="guardrail_tests") ∵ %6 [+]
read: The problem was that an independent test-author, writing tests blind from the spec, caught two §3 rules the guardrail never enforced: a silently-passing R:risk and an R:commit under a live fail-gate. The state was a guardrail that accepted graphs the spec forbids. The solution was to let the blind tests pin the contract, enforce the no-silent-pass and fail-gate-freeze rules, and lock them with regression tests before committing.

title: Completeness-critic found 'R:commit MUST be preceded by gate(ci:pass)' was unimplemented
problem: An adversarial completeness-critic found that the §3 rule 'R:commit MUST be preceded by gate(ci:pass)' (the merge-to-main classifier block) was not implemented at all.
state: The guardrail enforced the fail-gate freeze but never required an upstream gate(ci:pass) before a commit, so a commit with no CI evidence passed.
solution: The validate_commit_dataflow check was added to require a prior gate(ci:pass) before any executable R:commit line, and a regression test pinned it.
ail_v1:
[R:tool] %0 = read(path=tools/ailish/src/lib.rs)
[R:think] %1 = combine(%0, %0)
[R:review] %2 = join(%0, %1)
           gate(parse:pass) ∵ %2
[R:tool] %3 = edit(path=tools/ailish/src/lib.rs, fn="validate_commit_dataflow")
         %4 = test(target=%3)
[R:bench] %5 = test(target=%3) ; pass=8
          gate(ci:pass) ∵ %5
[R:artifact] gate(no_cjk:pass) ∵ %3
[R:commit] %6 = commit(scope="requires_ci_pass") ∵ %5
arp:
[STRIDE: read_impl → critic_review → find_gap → implement → regress → commit]
[T:70]
[R:tool] %0 = read(path=tools/ailish/src/lib.rs) [+]
[R:think] %1 = combine(%0, %0)
[R:review] %2 = join(%0, %1)
           gate(parse:pass) ∵ %2 [!]
[T:50]
[BRANCH: assume_complete | adversarial_recheck; condition: commit_prereq_unverified]
[R:tool] %3 = edit(path=tools/ailish/src/lib.rs, fn="validate_commit_dataflow") [+]
         %4 = test(target=%3)
[R:bench] %5 = test(target=%3) ; pass=8 [+]
          gate(ci:pass) ∵ %5
[R:artifact] gate(no_cjk:pass) ∵ %3
[T:10]
[R:commit] %6 = commit(scope="requires_ci_pass") ∵ %5 [+]
read: The problem was an adversarial completeness-critic discovering that the §3 prerequisite 'R:commit MUST be preceded by gate(ci:pass)' — the merge-to-main classifier block — was unimplemented. The state was a guardrail that froze on fail-gates but accepted commits with no CI evidence. The solution was to add the validate_commit_dataflow requires-ci-pass check and lock it with a regression test.

title: clippy -D warnings tripped by a doc-comment bullet list (doc_lazy_continuation)
problem: A doc-comment bullet list tripped clippy's doc_lazy_continuation lint under -D warnings, failing the build.
state: A correct-looking doc bullet list was parsed as a lazy continuation, so clippy promoted it to a hard error and blocked the commit.
solution: The doc comment was reflowed from a bullet list into prose, clearing the lint with zero warnings.
ail_v1:
[R:tool] %0 = build(target="clippy", flags="-D warnings")
[R:risk] %1 = check_permission(verb="commit", tool=`gh`) ; lint="doc_lazy_continuation"
         %2 = block(action="commit_with_warning", reason="lint_fail")
[R:tool] %3 = edit(path=tools/ailish/src/fmt.rs, change="reflow_doc_to_prose")
[R:bench] %4 = build(target=%3, kind="clippy") ; warnings=0
          gate(ci:pass) ∵ %4
[R:artifact] gate(english:pass) ∵ %3
[R:commit] %5 = commit(scope="doc_reflow") ∵ %4
arp:
[STRIDE: run_clippy → diagnose_lint → reflow_doc → reclippy → commit]
[T:60]
[R:tool] %0 = build(target="clippy", flags="-D warnings") [-]
[R:risk] %1 = check_permission(verb="commit", tool=`gh`) ; lint="doc_lazy_continuation" [-]
         %2 = block(action="commit_with_warning", reason="lint_fail")
[BRANCH: suppress_lint | reflow_doc_to_prose; condition: doc_bullet_list_tripped_lint]
[R:tool] %3 = edit(path=tools/ailish/src/fmt.rs, change="reflow_doc_to_prose") [+]
[R:bench] %4 = build(target=%3, kind="clippy") ; warnings=0 [+]
          gate(ci:pass) ∵ %4
[R:artifact] gate(english:pass) ∵ %3
[T:10]
[R:commit] %5 = commit(scope="doc_reflow") ∵ %4 [+]
read: The problem was that a doc-comment bullet list tripped clippy's doc_lazy_continuation lint under -D warnings and failed the build. The state was a blocked commit caused by a doc list clippy read as a lazy continuation. The solution was to reflow the doc comment into prose, which cleared the lint to zero warnings.

title: token_estimate: compact IS denser — the estimator splits registers on ':'
problem: There was a worry that the compact AI-lish form was not actually denser than the long form under the token estimator.
state: The compaction map looked like it might not save tokens, since both forms carry the same registers and operators.
solution: On noticing the estimator splits registers on ':' — so '[R:think]' costs 5 tokens while '[!T]' costs 3 — the compact form is confirmed strictly denser; this record is authored entirely in compact form.
ail_v1:
[!T] %0 = &(%1, %2)
     %3 = ^(%0, %1)
[!V] %4 = join(%0, %3)
     gate(parse:pass) ∵ %4
[!B] %5 = test(target=%4) ; tokens_long=5, tokens_compact=3
     gate(bench:pass) ∵ %5
[!A] gate(no_cjk:pass) ∵ %4
arp:
[STRIDE: estimate_long → estimate_compact → inspect_tokenizer → confirm_denser]
[T:65]
[!T] %0 = &(%1, %2)
     %3 = ^(%0, %1)
[!V] %4 = join(%0, %3)
     gate(parse:pass) ∵ %4 [!]
[BRANCH: compact_not_denser | compact_is_denser; condition: estimator_splits_register_on_colon]
[!B] %5 = test(target=%4) ; tokens_long=5, tokens_compact=3 [+]
     gate(bench:pass) ∵ %5
[T:15]
[!A] gate(no_cjk:pass) ∵ %4
read: The problem was a worry that the compact AI-lish form was not denser than the long form under the token estimator. The state was an apparent wash, since both forms carry the same registers and operators. The solution came from noticing the estimator splits registers on the colon, so '[R:think]' costs 5 tokens versus 3 for '[!T]', confirming the compact form is strictly denser.

title: SendMessage unavailable: orchestrator applied the precisely test-specified guardrail fix directly
problem: The orchestrator could not continue a subagent with its context (SendMessage was unavailable), so it could not hand the small guardrail fix back to the agent that scoped it.
state: The needed fix was small and precisely specified by an existing test, but there was no channel to resume the original subagent.
solution: Rather than re-spawn a fresh agent without context, the orchestrator applied the precisely test-specified guardrail fix directly as a gating reconciliation, then validated via the gate.
ail_v1:
[R:risk] %0 = check_permission(verb="launch_agent", tool=`gh`) ; state="sendmessage_unavailable"
         %1 = block(action="resume_subagent", reason="no_context_channel")
[R:think] %2 = combine(%0, %1)
[R:tool] %3 = edit(path=tools/ailish/src/lib.rs, change="precise_test_spec_fix")
         %4 = test(target=%3)
[R:bench] %5 = test(target=%3) ; pass=3
          gate(ci:pass) ∵ %5
[R:artifact] gate(english:pass) ∵ %3
[R:commit] %6 = commit(scope="gating_reconciliation") ∵ %5
arp:
[STRIDE: attempt_resume → detect_no_channel → mitigate → apply_direct_fix → gate → commit]
[T:70]
[R:risk] %0 = check_permission(verb="launch_agent", tool=`gh`) ; state="sendmessage_unavailable" [-]
         %1 = block(action="resume_subagent", reason="no_context_channel")
[BRANCH: resume_subagent_with_context | apply_specced_fix_directly; condition: sendmessage_unavailable]
[R:think] %2 = combine(%0, %1)
[T:45]
[R:tool] %3 = edit(path=tools/ailish/src/lib.rs, change="precise_test_spec_fix") [+]
         %4 = test(target=%3)
[R:bench] %5 = test(target=%3) ; pass=3 [+]
          gate(ci:pass) ∵ %5
[R:artifact] gate(english:pass) ∵ %3
[T:10]
[R:commit] %6 = commit(scope="gating_reconciliation") ∵ %5 [+]
read: The problem was that the orchestrator could not continue a subagent with its context because SendMessage was unavailable, leaving no way to hand back a small scoped guardrail fix. The state was a precisely test-specified fix with no channel to resume the original agent. The solution was to apply the fix directly as a gating reconciliation and validate it through the gate.

title: RFC 0009 merged mid-task: 'dense ARP graph' refined to validated-V1-core + behavioral primitives
problem: RFC 0009's normative ARP spec landed on main while the dataset was being built, changing what a 'dense ARP graph' formally means.
state: The in-progress dataset used a pre-RFC notion of a dense graph; the merged RFC defined it as a validated AI-lish V1 core plus the four behavioral primitives.
solution: The definition was reconciled against RFC 0009 and the record schema was realigned so every dense ARP graph is a validated V1 core with interleaved Stride/Tension/Valence/Branch annotations.
ail_v1:
[R:tool] %0 = fetch(remote="origin", ref="main")
         %1 = read(path=protocol/rfcs/0009-arp.md)
[R:think] %2 = combine(%0, %1)
[R:plan] depend(%2, %1) → target(action="realign_schema")
[R:review] %3 = join(%1, %2)
           gate(parse:pass) ∵ %3
[R:bench] %4 = build(target=%2, kind="schema") ; fields=10
          gate(ci:pass) ∵ %4
[R:artifact] gate(no_cjk:pass) ∵ %2
[R:commit] %5 = commit(scope="schema_realign") ∵ %4
arp:
[STRIDE: fetch_main → read_rfc → reconcile_definition → realign_schema → commit]
[T:60]
[R:tool] %0 = fetch(remote="origin", ref="main") [+]
         %1 = read(path=protocol/rfcs/0009-arp.md) [+]
[R:think] %2 = combine(%0, %1)
[BRANCH: keep_old_dense_definition | adopt_rfc0009_definition; condition: rfc0009_merged_mid_task]
[R:plan] depend(%2, %1) → target(action="realign_schema")
[R:review] %3 = join(%1, %2)
           gate(parse:pass) ∵ %3 [!]
[R:bench] %4 = build(target=%2, kind="schema") ; fields=10 [+]
          gate(ci:pass) ∵ %4
[R:artifact] gate(no_cjk:pass) ∵ %2
[T:15]
[R:commit] %5 = commit(scope="schema_realign") ∵ %4 [+]
read: The problem was RFC 0009's normative ARP spec merging to main mid-task, changing the formal meaning of a 'dense ARP graph.' The state was an in-progress dataset built on a pre-RFC notion of density. The solution was to reconcile against RFC 0009 and realign the record schema so every dense ARP graph is a validated V1 core with interleaved behavioral primitives.

title: Normalize-then-validate: V0-era tokens (⊕, ≈, ∅, {…}) rewritten into a valid V1 graph
problem: An expressive sample used V0-era tokens — ⊕, ≈, ∅, and set literals {…} — that AI-lish V1 rejects, so it failed to parse.
state: The sample was semantically meaningful but lexically pre-V1: math/set symbols and brace literals have no place in the V1 grammar.
solution: Normalize first, then validate: rewrite ⊕ to combine(), set literals to backtick symbols / intersect()/join(), and drop the rest, producing a graph that passes ailishcheck.
ail_v1:
[R:tool] %0 = read(path=samples/v0_expressive.ail)
[R:think] %1 = combine(%0, %0)
          %2 = intersect(%1, %0)
[R:review] %3 = join(%1, %2)
           gate(parse:pass) ∵ %3
[R:artifact] gate(no_cjk:pass) ∵ %3
[R:bench] %4 = test(target=%3) ; normalized=4
          gate(bench:pass) ∵ %4
arp:
[STRIDE: read_v0_sample → map_v0_tokens → normalize → validate_v1]
[T:55]
[R:tool] %0 = read(path=samples/v0_expressive.ail) [!]
[R:think] %1 = combine(%0, %0)
          %2 = intersect(%1, %0)
[BRANCH: reject_as_invalid | normalize_then_validate; condition: sample_uses_v0_era_tokens]
[R:review] %3 = join(%1, %2)
           gate(parse:pass) ∵ %3 [+]
[R:artifact] gate(no_cjk:pass) ∵ %3
[T:15]
[R:bench] %4 = test(target=%3) ; normalized=4 [+]
          gate(bench:pass) ∵ %4
read: The problem was an expressive sample written with V0-era tokens (⊕, ≈, ∅, set literals) that AI-lish V1 rejects, so it would not parse. The state was a meaningful but lexically pre-V1 graph full of math and set symbols. The solution was the normalize-then-validate discipline: rewrite ⊕ to combine(), set literals to symbols and intersect/join, and re-run ailishcheck until it passes.

title: Flaky CI: a transient network failure cleared on rerun, then a clean merge
problem: A CI run failed on a transient network timeout, not a real regression.
state: The red check blocked the merge even though the failure was non-deterministic infrastructure noise.
solution: Diagnose the failure as transient, rerun CI to a clean pass, then merge.
ail_v1:
[R:tool] %0 = read(path=ci/run.log)
[R:risk] %1 = check_permission(verb="merge", tool=`gh`) ; state="ci_red"
         %2 = block(action="merge_on_red", reason="ci_failed")
[R:think] %3 = combine(%0, %1)
[R:bench] %4 = test(target=%3) ; pass=12, rerun=true
          gate(ci:pass) ∵ %4
[R:artifact] gate(no_cjk:pass) ∵ %3
[R:commit] %5 = merge(pr=210) ∵ %4
arp:
[STRIDE: read_ci_log → diagnose_transient → rerun_ci → merge]
[T:70]
[R:tool] %0 = read(path=ci/run.log) [-]
[R:risk] %1 = check_permission(verb="merge", tool=`gh`) ; state="ci_red" [-]
[BRANCH: treat_as_regression | rerun_as_transient; condition: failure_is_network_timeout]
         %2 = block(action="merge_on_red", reason="ci_failed")
[R:think] %3 = combine(%0, %1)
[R:bench] %4 = test(target=%3) ; pass=12, rerun=true [+]
          gate(ci:pass) ∵ %4
[T:15]
[R:artifact] gate(no_cjk:pass) ∵ %3
[R:commit] %5 = merge(pr=210) ∵ %4 [+]
read: The problem was a CI run that failed on a transient network timeout rather than a real regression. The state was a red check blocking the merge despite the failure being non-deterministic infrastructure noise. The solution was to diagnose the failure as transient, rerun CI to a clean pass, and then merge.
